text.title: Ruby Sorbet Strict Floor with Tapioca RBI Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Every Ruby file the agent may modify autonomously MUST declare `# typed: true` (or stricter: strict / strong).
rule.rationale: Without a sigil, Sorbet ignores the file; agent edits propagate uncaught type errors.

rule.statement: RBIs for every gem (and Rails DSL) MUST be generated via `bundle exec tapioca` and committed under sorbet/rbi/.
rule.rationale: Without RBIs, Sorbet can't resolve gem methods; type checks are partial.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `bundle exec srb tc` and fail on any error.
rule.rationale: Local checks are skipped; CI is the gate.

rule.statement: CI MUST also run `bundle exec tapioca check-shims` to catch stale RBIs.
rule.rationale: Outdated RBIs let bugs through; the check ensures freshness.

rule.statement: T.unsafe / T.untyped usage MUST carry a comment justifying it; agents are forbidden from inserting it without one.
rule.rationale: Type escape hatches accumulate; named justifications make them reviewable.

rule.statement: If Ruby < 2.7 OR project relies on heavy meta-programming Sorbet can't model OR team has Steep/RBS, STOP.
rule.rationale: Forcing Sorbet over Steep / on incompatible Ruby creates noise without value.
